detaileddescription.para: the class stores some number of smallest elements from a larger number of candidates Generated from class MR::FewSmallest<MR::PointsProjectionResult>. Supported MR_PassBy modes: MR_PassBy_DefaultConstruct, MR_PassBy_Copy, MR_PassBy_Move (and MR_PassBy_DefaultArgument and MR_PassBy_NoObject if supported by the callee).

Constructs an empty (default-constructed) instance. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_FewSmallest_MR_PointsProjectionResult_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

Constructs an array of empty (default-constructed) instances, of the specified size. Will never return null. The array must be destroyed using MR_FewSmallest_MR_PointsProjectionResult_DestroyArray(). Use MR_FewSmallest_MR_PointsProjectionResult_OffsetMutablePtr() and MR_FewSmallest_MR_PointsProjectionResult_OffsetPtr() to access the array elements.

Offsets a pointer to an array element by i positions (not bytes). Use only if you're certain that the pointer points to an array element. The reference to the parameter ptr might be preserved in the return value.

Offsets a pointer to an array element by i positions (not bytes). Use only if you're certain that the pointer points to an array element. The reference to the parameter ptr might be preserved in the return value.

Generated from constructor MR::FewSmallest<MR::PointsProjectionResult>::FewSmallest. The reference to things referred to by the parameter _other (if any) might be preserved in the constructed object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_FewSmallest_MR_PointsProjectionResult_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

configure the object to store at most given number of elements Generated from constructor MR::FewSmallest<MR::PointsProjectionResult>::FewSmallest. The reference to the parameter maxElms might be preserved in the constructed object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_FewSmallest_MR_PointsProjectionResult_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

briefdescription.para: Destroys a heap-allocated instance of MR_FewSmallest_MR_PointsProjectionResult. Does nothing if the pointer is null.
Destroys a heap-allocated array of MR_FewSmallest_MR_PointsProjectionResult. Does nothing if the pointer is null.

detaileddescription.para: Generated from method MR::FewSmallest<MR::PointsProjectionResult>::operator=. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The reference to things referred to by the parameter _other (if any) might be preserved in this object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it. When this function is called, this object will drop any object references it had previously.

clears the content and reconfigure the object to store at most given number of elements Generated from method MR::FewSmallest<MR::PointsProjectionResult>::reset. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object.
returns the maximum number of elements to be stored here Generated from method MR::FewSmallest<MR::PointsProjectionResult>::maxElms. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object.
returns whether the container is currently empty Generated from method MR::FewSmallest<MR::PointsProjectionResult>::empty. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object.
returns current number of stored element Generated from method MR::FewSmallest<MR::PointsProjectionResult>::size. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object.
returns whether we have already maximum number of elements stored Generated from method MR::FewSmallest<MR::PointsProjectionResult>::full. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object.

returns the smallest elements found so far Generated from method MR::FewSmallest<MR::PointsProjectionResult>::get. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it.

returns the largest among stored smallest elements Generated from method MR::FewSmallest<MR::PointsProjectionResult>::top. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it.

returns the largest among stored smallest elements or given element if this is empty Generated from method MR::FewSmallest<MR::PointsProjectionResult>::topOr. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter emptyRes can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it.

considers one more element, storing it if it is within the smallest Generated from method MR::FewSmallest<MR::PointsProjectionResult>::push. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter t can not be null. It is a single object.
removes all stored elements Generated from method MR::FewSmallest<MR::PointsProjectionResult>::clear. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object.
